summary: No breaking API changes detected. All endpoint URLs, request parameters, and response schemas are unchanged from Sprint 7. The analytics module split is internal refactoring only — same prefix /analytics, same endpoint paths, same response schemas. Rate limit additions are transparent to clients (429 responses are new but clients must handle error responses). One new endpoint added: GET /analytics/fx-history with required query param validation (currency_pair allowlist added). No endpoints were removed or renamed.

checks.name: Endpoint URL changes
checks.detail: No endpoint URLs changed. Analytics module split is transparent — all /analytics/* paths still exist with same URLs.

checks.name: Response schema changes
checks.detail: No response schema changes. All existing endpoints return same shapes.

checks.name: New endpoints
checks.detail: GET /analytics/fx-history added (new feature, not a breaking change). GET /analytics/fx-gain-loss existed before, unchanged.

checks.name: Rate limit additions
checks.detail: 30/minute limits added to stocks, chart, alerts, watchlist endpoints. 429 responses are a new possible error code for these endpoints. Existing clients should handle 4xx errors — this is not a breaking change per API convention.

checks.name: backward-compat shim
checks.detail: app.api.analytics still exports invalidate_analytics_cache for callers that import from it (portfolios.py, sync.py). No import path changes needed.
